answer:
Zoho CRM APIs use OAuth 2.0 for authentication [1][2]. To access protected resources, you must obtain an access token using an authorization code (grant token) [3].

There are three primary ways to generate a grant token depending on your application type:

Web-based applications use a redirection-based flow where users log in via the Zoho login page [3].
Self-client options are used for standalone server-side applications or when no domain/redirect URL is available [3]. This is configured in the Zoho Developer Console [3][4].
Client Credentials flow is also supported for specific scenarios [3].

For API calls, the access token is passed in the authorization header:

```http
Authorization: Zoho-oauthtoken {access_token}
```

The SDKs streamline this by managing token persistence and refresh flows [2]. You initialize the SDK by providing your client ID, client secret, redirect URI, and the initial grant or refresh token [5][4][6][2].
